description:
This image displays a side-by-side comparison of Very High Resolution (VHR) satellite imagery, illustrating the input and output of a woody vegetation detection process. The left panel shows a natural-color satellite image of an agricultural and natural landscape. Visible features include cultivated fields (light brown, brownish-yellow), a main watercourse (river or large stream) in green, and numerous dark green linear features, which are likely riparian vegetation or field boundary hedges. Patches of scattered darker vegetation are also visible, particularly in the upper right quadrant. A road or track with a bridge crosses the watercourse.

The right panel shows a grayscale segmentation map derived from the input image. In this panel, white areas precisely correspond to the dark green linear features and the scattered vegetation patches observed in the left panel, representing detected woody vegetation. The rest of the landscape, including cultivated fields and the water body, is rendered in black, indicating the absence of detected woody vegetation. This output highlights Small Linear Features (SLF) and Small Woody Features (SWF) of vegetation, consistent with a trained model for woody vegetation detection from 0.5-2m/px high-resolution imagery.
